title: nuphar lutea subsp. variegata
intro: Nuphar lutea subsp. variegata, also known as the variegated yellow water lily or variegated spatterdock, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the water lily family. It is characterized by its unique variegated leaves and yellow flowers, and is commonly found in freshwater environments in Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
